title: Consumer and industrial marketing
summary: 9609 AS — B2C vs B2B marketing: buyers, decision process, and promotional approaches.
learningObjectives: Compare buyer behaviour in each market.

simpleExplanation.title: Selling to people vs businesses
simpleExplanation.summary: Consumer marketing targets individual buyers, often emotionally. Industrial (B2B) marketing targets organisations with rational, specification-driven purchasing.
steps: B2C: many buyers, small orders, emotional appeals.
B2B: few buyers, large orders, rational specs.
B2B uses personal selling and relationship marketing.
B2C uses mass advertising and branding.

flashcards.back: Business to consumer — final users buy for personal use.
Business to business — firms buy inputs, equipment, services for operations.
Fewer customers, larger order values, longer decision cycles, expert buyers.
B2B demand depends on consumer demand for final product (e.g. steel for cars).
Mass media advertising, brand image, emotional messaging.
Personal selling, trade shows, technical literature, negotiations.
Long-term contracts, trust, after-sales support critical.
CRM especially valuable in B2B for managing key accounts.

sections.content: **Consumer vs industrial marketing** (3.1.4) prevents **wrong mix recommendations** — do not suggest TV ads for industrial components or purely transactional selling for luxury B2C.

items: **B2C:** Large market, impulsive/emotional purchases, short decision, brand loyalty.
**B2B:** Derived demand, buying committees, specifications, price negotiations, repeat contracts.
**Promotion:** B2C mass media; B2B personal selling + technical detail.
**Price:** B2C fixed shelf price; B2B quotes, discounts, bulk terms.

sections.question: Compare marketing a smartphone (B2C) vs industrial microchips (B2B) — one difference in promotion and one in buyer behaviour.
sections.solution:
**Promotion:** Smartphone — **mass advertising**, influencers, retail display. Microchips — **direct sales team**, technical datasheets, trade fairs; no consumer TV ads.

**Buyer behaviour:** Smartphone buyer — **individual**, emotional brand choice, quick purchase. Chip buyer — **procurement team**, compares specs, reliability, price over **months**, long-term supply contracts.
